framework.name: BASE-100
framework.full_name: BASE-100 Global Standard for Business Maturity
framework.about: The Open Standard for Business Maturity

framework_description.summary: BASE-100 is an open, human-readable and machine-readable standard for measuring business maturity. It maps a company across 10 universal business domains and 11 maturity levels, from Level 0 to Level 10, creating a total score from 0 to 100.
framework_description.purpose: The purpose of BASE-100 is to help founders, operators, investors, advisors, and AI agents identify the current maturity of a business, detect its main constraint, and follow a clear path toward a more scalable, predictable, and transferable company.
framework_description.core_thesis: A business is not just a creative project. It is also an engineered system. Most of the work required to run a valuable company is universal, measurable, and improvable.
framework_description.design_principle: BASE-100 standardizes the universal operating core of a company so that leadership can focus on the differentiated edge where true market advantage is created.
what_it_is: A shared context layer for humans and AI
what_it_is_not: A promise that every company becomes a unicorn

positioning.primary_outcome: To transform a founder-dependent business into a scalable, governable, and transferable asset.

governance_and_curation.governance_model: Annual revision by the BASE-100 Institute with expert review and public commentary.
governance_and_curation.expert_review: Each milestone in the framework is reviewed against real-world operating practice, auditability, and relevance across multiple industries.
governance_and_curation.annual_revision_principle: The standard is revised annually so that the required level of maturity reflects current business reality rather than outdated best practice.

change_control.backward_compatibility_goal: High, unless a definition is no longer defensible or useful.

operating_beliefs: Maturity is not the same as size.
A larger company can be less mature than a smaller one.
A company becomes more valuable when it becomes less dependent on individual heroics.
The weakest important constraint limits the performance of the whole system.
Good judgment is strengthened by evidence, not replaced by opinion.
Standardization should reduce friction, not remove creativity.
A company becomes more transferable as its logic becomes more explicit.

important_limits: BASE-100 measures operational maturity, not market size.
BASE-100 does not replace legal, tax, regulatory, or industry-specific compliance frameworks.
BASE-100 does not determine whether the product is good enough to win its market.
BASE-100 is strongest when used together with operator judgment and market reality.

definitions.business: A legal and operational entity that creates and delivers value in exchange for money.
definitions.domain: One of the 10 universal areas of business operation measured by the framework.
definitions.cell: The intersection of one domain and one maturity level.
definitions.level: A maturity state from 0 to 10 within a domain.
definitions.maturity: The degree to which a business activity is designed, organized, measured, improved, and independent of specific individuals.
definitions.constraint: The domain-level weakness currently limiting the performance, scalability, or transferability of the business.
definitions.evidence: A verifiable artifact that proves a maturity claim is true.
definitions.audit: The process of checking whether a maturity claim is supported by acceptable evidence.
definitions.artifact: A document, system record, workflow log, dashboard, policy, template, or other observable proof.
definitions.operator: A person responsible for running part or all of the business.
definitions.founder_dependence: A condition in which important business activity requires direct founder intervention to function reliably.
definitions.transferable: Able to continue operating successfully under a new owner or leadership structure.
definitions.product: A repeatable unit of value delivered to a customer, whether physical, digital, or service-based.
definitions.offer: A clearly defined commercial package combining product or service, target customer, scope, and pricing logic.
definitions.lead: A documented prospect who has shown relevant commercial interest.
definitions.customer: A person or entity that has already paid for an offer.
definitions.SOP: A written standard operating procedure describing how a recurring task is completed.
definitions.KPI: A numeric metric used to measure performance against an expected result.
definitions.command_center: A live view of key business metrics across domains.
definitions.source_of_truth: The approved system or document where the official current version of information lives.
definitions.review_frequency: How often a cell should be checked to confirm it remains true.

maturity_model.scoring_unit: One point per validated cell level per domain
maturity_model.interpretation: Each domain is scored from 0 to 10. Total maturity is the sum of all 10 domain scores.
maturity_model.important_note: A high total score does not cancel out a critical weakness in a core domain.

0.name: Designed
0.definition: The domain has a written design, intent, and success definition.

1.name: Defined
1.definition: The core object, process, or rule exists in basic written form.

2.name: Organized
2.definition: Information is stored in one place with basic order and operational hygiene.

3.name: Owned
3.definition: A named person owns the domain outcome and visibility exists.

4.name: Standardized
4.definition: The work follows a defined method, template, or repeatable process.

5.name: Measured
5.definition: Numeric targets and regular reporting exist.

6.name: Proactive
6.definition: The business prevents problems through planning, forecasting, or structured intervention.

7.name: Integrated
7.definition: The domain works in sync with other domains through shared workflows, systems, or decision rules.

8.name: Improving
8.definition: The domain systematically improves through feedback loops and regular refinement.

9.name: Independent
9.definition: The domain continues performing with low key-person risk.

10.name: Transferable
10.definition: The domain is highly autonomous, governable, and ready for external transfer or institutional oversight.

stage_model.note: Stages are descriptive groupings of typical business maturity, not hard requirements by size.

discovery.focus: Problem-solution fit and initial business design

validation.focus: Repeatable sales and basic operating control

systemization.focus: Documented management, repeatability, and proactive control

scoring_principles.base_rule: A cell is only counted when the evidence standard is met.
scoring_principles.evidence_rule: A verbal claim is not enough. A maturity claim must be supported by a current artifact.
scoring_principles.freshness_rule: Evidence should be current enough to prove the business still operates at that level.
scoring_principles.constraint_rule: A weak core domain can limit the practical value of strength in other domains.
scoring_principles.anti-vanity_rule: Activity is not maturity. A company does not score higher merely because it is busy or uses many tools.
scoring_principles.copy_rule: Buying software does not count unless the process is actually implemented and adopted.
scoring_principles.inheritance_rule: Higher levels should not contradict lower levels. If a lower foundational level has clearly collapsed, higher claims should be reviewed.
scoring_principles.review_rule: A maturity level must remain true over time, not only on the day of an audit.

minimum_evidence_quality: Accessible to the people who use it
non_evidence_examples: A verbal explanation with no artifact
A blank template never used in practice
A document that exists but is outdated and ignored
A tool subscription with no implementation
A one-time action presented as a system

audit_model.audit_style: Binary by cell, judgment by system
audit_model.binary_rule: Each cell should be written so the main audit question can be answered yes or no.
audit_model.partial_use: Partial may be used internally during drafting, but the published standard should aim for pass/fail clarity.

field_definitions.headline: Short name of the maturity milestone.
field_definitions.plain_english: Simple explanation understandable by non-native operators.
field_definitions.observation: The operating reality that should exist at this level.
field_definitions.why_it_matters: Business reason this milestone increases quality, scalability, or transferability.
field_definitions.audit_binary: Main yes/no question used to validate the level.
field_definitions.evidence_example: What acceptable evidence looks like in practice.
field_definitions.example: A 90% best-practice implementation example that can be copied without naming brands.
field_definitions.common_fake_good: A misleading version that looks mature but is not.
field_definitions.failure_signal: Observable real-world sign that the level is not actually achieved.
field_definitions.prerequisite: The cell or condition that should exist first, or null if none.

weights_schema.impact: 1-10 impact on value, profit potential, or system strength

writing_rules.rule_1: Every cell must be understandable by a competent non-native English operator.
writing_rules.rule_2: Every cell must describe a visible operating reality, not a vague aspiration.
writing_rules.rule_3: Every audit question must be verifiable in a practical audit.
writing_rules.rule_4: Every evidence field must name a concrete artifact.
writing_rules.rule_5: Every example must be tool-agnostic and copyable.
writing_rules.rule_6: Every failure signal must describe what broken reality looks like.
writing_rules.rule_7: Every level must align with the universal level logic.
writing_rules.rule_8: Avoid industry-specific assumptions unless clearly marked.
writing_rules.rule_9: Avoid magic numbers unless they are broadly defensible or explicitly illustrative.
writing_rules.rule_10: Level 10 should be aspirational but still defensible.

recommended_usage_order: Prioritize the next few highest-leverage cells
Use the framework as a roadmap, not just a score
implementation_guidance.important_note: Companies should not try to complete every possible improvement at once. The framework is most useful when it helps leadership focus on the next constraint.

extensions.name: Industry Implementation Packs
extensions.description: Optional guidance layers for sector-specific implementation without changing the core standard.

extensions.name: Audit Profiles
extensions.description: Different audit depth levels for self-assessment, advisory review, lender review, or investor due diligence.

extensions.name: AI Context Profiles
extensions.description: Structured operating context for AI agents working inside the business.

extensions.name: Benchmarking Layers
extensions.description: Future comparative datasets by company type, maturity stage, and operating model.

known_limitations: A mature company can still fail if the market is wrong.
A great product can still be run through immature operations.
Some industry-specific controls must be added outside the base standard.
The framework measures maturity, not genius, timing, or luck.
The framework is strongest when used repeatedly over time, not once.

future_domain_structure.placeholder: Domains to be completed one by one in later revisions.
